channel.title: Articles on Bali Kini
channel.description: Recent content in Articles on Bali Kini

item.title: Bali Tourism June 2026: 553K Visitors in April, Target 6.6M for the Year
item.description:
Bali welcomed 553,000 foreign visitors in April 2026, according to the latest data from BPS Bali (Statistics Indonesia). While this represents a 6.41% decline year-on-year, the island’s tourism economy tells a more nuanced story.

The Visitor Paradox

Despite fewer international arrivals, hotel occupancy rates across Bali actually increased during the same period. Analysts attribute this anomaly to a surge in domestic tourism — Indonesian travelers from Java and other islands filling the rooms that fewer foreign visitors left empty.

item.title: Balinale 2026: 94 Films from 38 Countries Illuminate Sanur
item.description:
Sanur, Bali has become the center of the international film world’s attention. The Bali International Film Festival, widely known as Balinale, is celebrating its 19th edition from June 1–7, 2026. Ninety-four films from 38 countries are being screened across multiple venues in the Sanur area, cementing Balinale’s position as one of Southeast Asia’s most prestigious film festivals.

Theme and Spirit of the 19th Edition

Carrying the theme “Stories Worth Experiencing”, Balinale 2026 arrives with a spirit of celebrating the power of cinema as a universal storytelling medium. This edition is particularly significant: Balinale has been officially recognized as an Academy Award®-qualifying film festival — the only one in Indonesia — meaning every short film award carries real weight in the global film industry.
